title: Fix post-login routing: skip character creation when character already exists

requirements.summary: After login and realm selection, query backend for existing character and skip CharacterCreationScreen if character exists, routing directly to character sheet/dungeon select screen
acceptanceCriteria: After login, if a character exists for the selected realm, the CharacterCreationScreen is never rendered and the player lands on the character sheet or dungeon select screen.
After login, if no character exists for the selected realm, the CharacterCreationScreen is shown as normal.
This behavior applies to both Softcore and Hardcore realm selections.
No 'Actor Not Available' or unhandled error is shown when a character already exists.

file_operations.description: Add logic immediately after realm selection to query the backend for an existing character using the getCharacter() method. If a character exists for the selected realm in the current season, set the screen state to skip CharacterCreationScreen and route directly to the character sheet or dungeon select screen. Only show CharacterCreationScreen when getCharacter() returns null for the selected realm. Ensure this check happens before any screen rendering decisions and handle the query loading state appropriately to prevent flickering.

requirements.summary: Handle #alreadyExists error from createCharacter by silently redirecting to character sheet/dungeon select screen instead of showing error
acceptanceCriteria: If createCharacter returns #alreadyExists, no error toast or error screen is shown.
The player is automatically routed to the character sheet or dungeon select screen.
The app does not crash or enter an unrecoverable state.

file_operations.description: Update the character creation success/error handling in App.tsx to detect when createCharacter returns a Result with err variant of type CharacterCreationError.alreadyExists. When this specific error is detected, silently redirect the player to the character sheet or dungeon select screen without displaying any error message or toast. Ensure the app gracefully handles this race condition scenario and does not crash or show confusing error states.

Update the useCreateCharacter mutation hook to properly type and expose the CharacterCreationError variants (including alreadyExists) from the backend Result type, allowing App.tsx to distinguish between different error cases. Ensure the mutation result properly surfaces the error variant so the calling code can handle #alreadyExists differently from other errors.
